previousChapter.title: Product & Technology
previousChapter.mission: Explain what is delivered, how it works, maturity, dependencies, and product/technical risks.
contentRequirements: Product/service definition in customer workflow terms.
Module, SKU, asset, pipeline, facility, or product-line map.
Architecture or operating model — software stack, hardware design, manufacturing, clinical mechanism, logistics, or service workflow.
Deployment, integration, reliability, support, and roadmap.
Differentiation — technology, IP, data, manufacturing know-how, supply access, brand, regulatory approvals, or operations.
Trust, safety, security, privacy, compliance, and quality controls.

plannedTables.name: Product module / asset matrix
plannedTables.requirement: module/asset/product line, user, status/maturity, differentiation, diligence gap.

plannedTables.name: Workflow / use-case table
plannedTables.requirement: user job, current workflow, company solution, measurable benefit, limitation.

plannedFigures.name: Customer workflow / operating flow
plannedFigures.requirement: How the product/service is used or delivered.

plannedFigures.name: Critical dependency map
plannedFigures.requirement: Suppliers, platforms, data rights, regulators, facilities, or key partners.

evidenceStrategy: Official-surface mining is mandatory when a company URL exists — homepage, sitemap, product/docs/API, changelog, integrations, pricing, trust/security, status, certifications, customer stories.
Technical proof from patents, papers, clinical/trial records, certifications, regulatory databases, benchmarks, status pages.
External proof from reviews, customer deployments, partner announcements, regulator filings, recalls/incidents.

Developer-signal (sourceType `developer-signal`) is required — GitHub repo activity (stars, releases, contributors), Hacker News threads, Stack Overflow tag traffic, public Discord/Slack metrics, package registry downloads, npm/PyPI/HuggingFace hub stats, API forums.

For companies without a public developer surface (hardware, manufacturing, clinical, regulated industries), satisfy `developer-signal` via the closest practitioner-community proxy — industry forums, professional-society discussions, integration partner posts, public engineer recruiting / talks, or a documented note in `evidenceGaps[]` explaining why no proxy exists.
Do not infer architecture, approvals, certifications, throughput, or safety from vague marketing copy.
qualityBar: Distinguish verified maturity, roadmap claims, integrations, and certifications from marketing.
Make architecture and workflow figures specific, not generic boxes.
Surface product, security, reliability, regulatory, supply, or implementation gaps.
Each module row states maturity/status, buyer/user, differentiation, and diligence gap.
Unsupported architecture, certifications, integrations, performance, or roadmap claims become evidenceGaps.
Figures use structured YAML fields, not Mermaid or prose diagrams.

chapter.title: Customers
chapter.mission: Identify who pays/uses the product, the proof of real adoption, durability, expansion, and concentration risks.
contentRequirements: Customer base segmentation by buyer/user/payer, geography, vertical, size, channel, use case, or revenue band.
Adoption trajectory (active usage, repeat purchase, deployment, accounts, locations, utilization) where supportable.
Named customer proof — production vs pilot, outcomes, reference quality, evidence freshness.
Retention/durability — NRR, GRR, churn, renewal, contract length, cohorts, satisfaction, or gaps.
Expansion and concentration — land-and-expand, top-customer risk, channel/partner dependence, procurement friction.

plannedTables.name: Customer segmentation table
plannedTables.requirement: segment, buyer/user/payer, use case, scale, revenue/strategic value, gap.

plannedTables.name: Customer growth / adoption trajectory table
plannedTables.requirement: metric, value, date, source, confidence, implication, missing denominator.

plannedTables.name: Named customer proof table
plannedTables.requirement: customer, segment, deployment/use case, production vs pilot, outcome, limitation.

plannedTables.name: Retention / repeat usage / satisfaction table
plannedTables.requirement: metric, value/null, segment, confidence, diligence ask.

plannedTables.name: Expansion and concentration risk table
plannedTables.requirement: expansion driver, concentration risk, impact, diligence path.

plannedFigures.name: Customer journey map
plannedFigures.requirement: Customer segments, adoption surfaces, and expansion loops.

plannedFigures.name: Customer proof matrix
plannedFigures.requirement: Evidence quality, outcome specificity, retention visibility, or production maturity.

plannedFigures.name: Retention / repeat cohort
plannedFigures.requirement: Time-series retention only — columns are time buckets (e.g. month-1/3/6 or year-1/2) and cell values are retention percentages 0–100.

evidenceStrategy: Official customer evidence — customer pages, case studies, testimonials, partner stories, marketplaces; logos only when tied to use.
Independent evidence — procurement/tender records, reviews, marketplace ratings, public customer announcements, filings, analyst reports.
Adverse evidence — churn/complaints, poor reviews, failed deployments, outages, procurement blockers, lawsuits, concentration warnings.

Customer-proof (sourceType `customer-proof`) is required — G2/Capterra/Gartner Peer Insights reviews, named case studies, customer-quoted press releases, LinkedIn testimonials, conference talks by named users, government procurement records.
Separate broad customer-count claims from named deployments and retention proof.
qualityBar: Distinguish logos, named deployments, active use, outcomes, retention, and expansion.
Tie customer evidence to buyer/user/payer segments and revenue or strategic value.
Convert unsupported retention, customer count, satisfaction, or concentration claims into null plus exact diligence asks.
Include adverse searches for churn, complaints, blocked deployments, concentration, failed pilots.
Logos alone do not prove retention or production deployment.

nextChapter.title: Risks
nextChapter.mission: Rank legal, regulatory, operational, technical, financial, partner, customer, and execution risks with mitigations and kill criteria.
contentRequirements: Severity-ranked risks with likelihood, impact, mitigation maturity, residual exposure, and investment implication.
Regulatory/legal risk — licenses, approvals, litigation, enforcement, IP, privacy, environmental/safety.
Operational risk — supply chain, manufacturing, logistics, reliability, outages, recalls, safety, facilities, labor.
Partner/dependency risk — supplier, distributor, cloud/platform, capital provider, regulator, key customer concentration.
Financial/model risk — burn, capital intensity, credit/loss exposure, fraud, working capital, margin compression.
Mitigations, monitoring indicators, thesis-break triggers, and diligence asks.

plannedTables.name: Regulatory / legal risk register
plannedTables.requirement: rule/license/case, jurisdiction, status, likelihood, severity, mitigation, residual exposure, diligence path; rows ordered by severity.

plannedTables.name: Operational / quality / security risk register
plannedTables.requirement: failure mode, likelihood, severity, mitigation maturity, residual exposure, unresolved gap; rows ordered by severity.

plannedTables.name: Partner / dependency risk register
plannedTables.requirement: dependency, counterparty, role, concentration, failure scenario, severity, mitigation, residual exposure; rows ordered by severity.

plannedTables.name: People / execution risk register
plannedTables.requirement: role/function, dependency or gap, likelihood, severity, mitigation, diligence path; rows ordered by severity.

plannedTables.name: Mitigation and kill criteria table
plannedTables.requirement: risk, monitorable trigger, threshold/event, action implication.

plannedFigures.name: Risk heatmap
plannedFigures.requirement: Impact, likelihood, mitigation maturity, or residual severity.

plannedFigures.name: Risk transmission map
plannedFigures.requirement: How risks flow into revenue, customers, margin, financing, operations, and valuation.

plannedFigures.name: Dependency map
plannedFigures.requirement: Critical partners, suppliers, platforms, regulators, facilities, or financing dependencies.

evidenceStrategy: Official — trust/security/privacy/terms/DPA/compliance/status/incident pages, certifications, recall and safety pages.
Primary sources — regulators, court records, clinical/regulatory databases, patent/IP offices, environmental agencies, sanctions/export-control sources.
Independent — adverse reporting, security incident coverage, customer complaints, analyst risk notes, comparable risk factors.
qualityBar: Cite evidence and mitigation for every top risk; never assign severity from intuition.
Define monitorable kill criteria or diligence paths for unresolved risks.
When litigation, regulatory posture, certifications, incidents, safety, or concentration cannot be confirmed, record exact diligence path.
Include thesis-break triggers tied to measurable operating, regulatory, customer, financial, or management events.
